entry.title: Re: ATO for Sales Quotation
entry.content:
Hi ahmedramadan,

AATP is working per default with temporary quantity assignment (TQA) when you trigger the ATP check via a sales document (e.g. for a sales quotation via VA22). This is can not/should not be changed to prevent false results from ATP check.

You can however run an ATP simulation call via API (e.g. API_AVAIL_TO_PROMISE_CHECK).

Hope this helps.

best regards,

Michael

entry.title: Re: Approval Process for Sales Orders S4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

Setting up an approval process for sales orders when a net value condition is involved requires several configuration steps. Since you’ve already taken steps to define the approval request, set up the workflow, and create custom logic, let’s consider what might be missing or incorrectly configured based on your reported issues:

1. Ensure Approval Reasons and Workflow Conditions Match the Custom Logic

  • Verify that the reasons for approval requests you have defined match the conditions in your custom logic. The same sales document type and condition checks should be present in both the BAdI implementation and the workflow settings.

2. Check Configuration of Team Members and Their Roles

  • Make sure that the team members and roles for approving the sales orders are correctly defined in the ‘Manage Teams and Responsibilities’ app. The approvers should have the necessary authorization to view and process approval requests.

3. Verify Email and Notification Settings

  • Confirm that email addresses are correctly set up in the ‘Maintain Workforce’ app. Notifications should be configured to ensure automated emails are sent when an approval request is initiated. Emails and notifications should be generated based on the templates you have configured.

4. Role and User Assignments in Workflow

  • Ensure that the correct role assignment is present in the workflow setup. The recipient of the approval request should be accurately defined with proper permissions to receive and act upon approval requests in their inbox.

5. Check Exception Handling and Notification Tab

  • Ensure exception handling is properly set to deal with rework steps (if applicable) and verify that the notification configurations are active to send alerts when tasks are overdue or pending.

6. Test and Adjust Workflow Activation

  • Finally, validate that the workflow is activated post-configuration. Only active workflows will trigger approval tasks in the user’s inbox. Ensure all defined conditions in the workflow are correctly set and align with the setup logic.

Conclusion

By reviewing these areas, you should be able to identify any missed configuration or incorrect setups that might be causing your approval workflow to not function correctly.

For further guidance, consult the SAP Help Portal Sales - Setup Example: Approval Workflows in Your System or advisory notes related to approval workflows.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Ariba: Invoice issue
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Need your suggestion on the below mentioned issue which we are facing in our test environment.

The invoices for Indirect POs are failing in VIM because of "Invalid PO Item Number".

Reason for this is in SBN, the Indirect PO item interval is 1,2,3,...

But the SAP ZARB PO item interval is 10,20,30,.... 

When Supplier creates the Invoice in SBN, it adopts the item numbering from the SBN PO (1,2,3,...).  When that invoice makes it to SAP for processing, VIM is checking for that PO # and Line # in SAP and can't find a match since the SAP PO has different item numbering (10,20,30,...).

Since this is a standard process do we need any customization?

Do we require any of the option's to overcome this issue (1) CIG mapping solution or (2) change ZARB line item interval config to 1,2,3.If so kindly suggest what needs to be done.

entry.title: Almosafer's Strategic Leap: Digital Transformation and Growth in Emerging Markets
entry.content:
In the intricate world of business, particularly in sectors highly influenced by cultural dimensions such as tourism and travel, the art of localization is essential. Almosafer, a leading travel and tourism enterprise in the Middle East, exemplifies how embracing localization is not just a strategic move, but a necessary evolution to stay relevant and compliant in a global landscape.

The Growth and Localization Journey

Operating under Seera Group, Almosafer, Saudi Arabia’s leading travel company is elevating the journey for travellers from Saudi Arabia, the region & beyond, while harnessing Seera Group’s 40+ years of expertise to align with the national vision for tourism. The company creates diverse travel opportunities, serving various markets with state-of-the-art solutions and a digital-first mindset.

Leading these digital transformation efforts is Mr. Usman Khan, the Senior Manager of Enterprise Technology. His extensive experience, coupled with his deep understanding of SAP technology and best practices, has been critical in streamlining operations and aligning them with business goals since he joined the team six years ago.

One of the key areas of focus for the company’s growth strategy has been the localization of its operations. The organization's commitment to adapting its services to meet the specific needs of different markets has been instrumental in establishing Almosafer as a leader in the travel services industry, both within the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and globally.

Strategic localization efforts are evident in the expansion of travel platforms and providing tailored solutions for both individual and corporate clients. By investing in partnerships with local and international stakeholders, Almosafer has been able to offer a diverse range of services. These include travel activities, destination management, and Hajj & Umrah arrangements, all designed to cater to the unique needs of different customer segments.

Embracing E-Invoicing in Saudi Arabia's Digital Transformation

Gone are the days when a company's financial operations were buried under heaps of paper. In an era marked by technological advances and digital convergence, e-invoicing has emerged as a revolutionary regulatory and business practice. It's a transformative solution that is reshaping business operations across various market verticals.

The transition to e-invoicing is a cornerstone of Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030, signifying a commitment to fostering a robust digital economy. With the mandate effective from December 2021, all business enterprises within the Kingdom are obliged to transition to electronic invoicing. This initiative transcends mere digitization of paper; it fundamentally aims to enhance transparency, increase efficiency, and streamline business operations across diverse sectors.

With its redefined identity Almosafer offers a comprehensive digital experience to their customers, now enjoying the convenience of booking flights and hotels online, which simplifies travel planning and provides a seamless, user-friendly interface.

Championing Localization and Operational Transparency

Almosafer’s commitment to digital transformation is not only evident in their compliance with Saudi Arabia’s e-invoicing regulations but also in their strategic operational advancements. By introducing e-invoicing, led by Mr. Usman Khan and his team, the company has not only aligned with the Kingdom’s regulatory framework but has also revolutionized its operational practices to enhance transparency and efficiency. This dedication to compliance and improved operations cultivates deeper trust and reliability amongst their clientele.

The strategic partnership with SAP has been pivotal in, adept navigation through Saudi Arabia's complex legal landscape by aligning with the Zakat and Tax Authority (ZATCA)'s rigorous regulations and fostering greater trust and reliability amongst Almosafer’s customers. By implementing SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, the company not only met the kingdom’s FATOORAH invoicing requirements but also ensured all processes are transparent, controlled, and compliant, crucially supporting the company’s ongoing commitment to regulatory adherence and enhancing operational excellence.

Furthermore, a core element of their strategy includes the localization of their invoicing systems, which is tailored uniquely to meet regional language preferences and address specific market nuances. This proactive approach has done more than simply meet local needs; it has cemented Almosafer's leadership within these markets. By ensuring every transaction is transparent and traceable, this localization strategy has deepened customer trust and enhanced the company’s standing as a reliable and adaptable market leader.

Exploring the depth of Almosafer's strategic endeavors, it is clear that the integration of e-invoicing is not merely about compliance but is a vital step toward enhancing operational transparency and efficiency. The thoughtful localization of their invoicing systems, customized to meet regional demands and market nuances, affirms their commitment to adapting to and anticipating customer needs. Echoing Mr. Khan’s vision, these efforts are part of a broader strategy to 'redefine what's possible in the travel and tourism sector.' The company's proactive embrace of digital transformation illustrates a thriving enterprise setting a benchmark in the digital age.  This narrative not only highlights the essential role of advanced technologies like e-invoicing in modern business operations but also positions Almosafer as a model of innovation and leadership in the global marketplace. 

Summary

The story of Almosafer exemplifies the transformative power of digital integration in meeting regulatory demands and driving significant operational improvements within an industry. As industries around the globe continue to navigate the complexities of digital transformation, the stands as a beacon, showing that with the right approach and technologies, businesses

Insights from Mr. Khan highlight the critical role of SAP technology in Almosafer's success, reflecting a model that balances innovation with practical execution. This narrative underscores the significance of adopting digital innovations that cater to specific market needs and setting new benchmarks in industry leadership. Almosafer's story serves as a compelling blueprint for businesses worldwide, aiming to leverage digital capabilities for sustainable growth and competitive superiority in the digital age.

Learn about Localization Today 

Localization Capabilities on sap.com 

SAP Community for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

SAP Community for Localization

entry.title: 新Solmanに存在しない標準ユーザについて
entry.content:
ご担当者様

お世話になっております。

新しいSolution Managerを構築しました。

旧環境と新環境のSolution Managerのバージョンは以下の通りです。

旧環境：Solution Manager7.2 SP8

新環境：Solution Manager7.2 SP19

標準ユーザを旧環境と新環境で比較したところ、旧環境に存在していて、新環境に存在しないユーザが１０存在しました。

これらのユーザは自動で生成されるユーザと認識していますが、新環境に存在しないのはバージョンの違いによるものでしょうか。

SLDAPIUSER
SLDDSUSER
SLD_CS_USER
SLD_DS_SPJ
SLD_DS_SPJ2
SMB_SPD00001
SMD_ADMIN
SM_ADMIN_SPD
SOLMANSPD100
SOLTMWSPD100

以上、よろしくお願いします


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: 新Solmanに存在しない標準ユーザについて
entry.content:
ご担当者　様

平素よりお世話になっております。

貴社のシステムを確認させていただいたところ、SLD（System Landscape Directory）を使用しない構成を選択されており、ランドスケープデータは直接 LMDB（Landscape Management Database）に提供されていることが分かりました。

そのため、SLD と LMDB 間のデータ同期に関連する以下のユーザーは作成されません。詳細はTechnical User for LMDB and SLDをご参考お願い致します：

  • SLD_DS_SPJ / SLD_DS_SPJ2（SLD データサプライヤが技術システム情報を SLD に書き込むために必要）

  • SLDAPIUSER（中央 SLD が存在する場合、SAP Solution Manager のインストール時に作成されます）

  • SLDDSUSER

  • SLD_CS_USER（SLD システム上に存在し、System Landscape 情報を SLD から収集するために使用）

また、SMD_ADMIN ユーザーについては、各 Diagnostics Agent が Solution Manager の Java スタックに対して P4 接続を確立します。Solution Manager 7.1 SP05 まではこの接続は「標準認証接続」であり、一般的に SMD_ADMIN ユーザーが使用されておりましたが、現在では使用されておりません。詳細はAdministrator User in Java: SM_ADMIN_<SolManSID>をご参考お願い致します

さらに、SM_ADMIN_<SolManSID> ユーザーは、SAP Solution Manager によりマネージドシステムをセットアップする際に、Java 管理ユーザーとして手動で作成する必要があります。このユーザーは、マネージドシステム上での他のユーザーの作成、ロールの割り当て、および診断のセルフチェック実行を担います。

なお、SOLTMWSPD100ユーザーは、以前は Solution Manager 7.1 における ChaRM（Change Request Management）機能で使用されていましたが、現在では使用されておりません。SOLMAN<SID><client> ユーザーは、かつて使用されていた RFC 宛先用の技術的な通信ユーザーでもありますが、現在では使用されておりません。 

以上、ご参照のほど、よろしくお願いします。

宜しくお願い致します。

SAPサポート

entry.title: Re: ME2O/ME2ON does not extracts Reservations for MRP Area
entry.content:
Normally, you should create a New MRP Area in the background configuration, then assign the MRP Area to the material, and you can find the data in Table MDMA for the material. 

For reservations related to MRP areas, especially in subcontracting contexts: Stock transfers for components to be provided to subcontractors can create reservations with movement type 541.

These reservations are created during the MRP run when: An MRP area is set up for the subcontractor Components are assigned to the subcontractor's MRP area .A special procurement key for stock transfer from plant to MRP area is defined Source list entries are properly configured .

To view these reservations: Use transaction ME2O (SC Stock Monitoring for Vendor) This transaction allows you to display the reservations for components to be provided to subcontractors 

For general MRP area-related information: Transaction MD04 is more suitable for viewing MRP area details It can show both plant and subcontracting MRP areas, depending on the material configuration.

And you can check the Help link as bellow.

Stock Transfer of Components to be Provided | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: ME2O/ME2ON does not extracts Reservations for MRP Area
entry.content:
"Hi DerekDing, thank you for your response.

Based on what you're saying, and the evidence from my tests, in order to see the transfer requirements from the Division to the MRP Area in ME2O/ME2ON, I definitely need to create the material master record on the MRP Area (entry in MDMA).

On the other hand, according to the resources I linked in my original question, this should not be necessary.

The purpose of my question is this: to understand if there is an inconsistency between the documentation and the behavior of the SAP Standard, or if I have omitted some configuration myself.

I recall that in my case, the transfer requirements are always created correctly by MRP (both with and without the records in MD04). The only difference is the extraction of these requirements by ME2O/ME2ON.

    • Gaurish Dessai (Principal Enterprise Architect, SAP)

📅 Session 2: Unleash Your Data and AI with Irfan Khan and Timo Elliott

We are joined by Irfan Khan to talk about SAP’s Data and AI strategy across its Business Applications. Irfan will then join Timo for a fireside chat to answer questions from the audience and explore how SAP’s latest data and AI strategies can supercharge your organization’s future.

  • When: June 5, 2025, at 11 AM EST / 5 PM CET
  • Speakers:
    • Paul Kurchina
    • Irfan Khan (President & Chief Product Officer, SAP Data & Analytics)
    • Timo Elliott (Global Innovation Evangelist & VP Marketing at SAP)

📅 Session 3: Unpacking SAP Business Data Cloud for Enterprise Architects

Next, we unravel SAP Business Data Cloud and Databricks capabilities for Architects and understand what’s under the hood. We do a technical deep dive into how SAP BDC fits into your landscape. In this session, we will provide architects with a complete view of SAP’s new data offering called SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC).  SAP BDC allows SAP customers to bring their SAP + Non-SAP and Structured + Unstructured Data together in one platform that unifies data governance, analytics, and AI. We will deep-dive on two new concepts:

  1. Data Products which enable governance and sharing organizations data assets while preserving the business context
  2. Intelligent Applications which bring together data analytics and embedded AI together.
  • When: June 12, 2025, at 11 AM EST / 5 PM CET
  • Speakers:
    • Ralph Richter (Chief Enterprise Architect, SAP)
    • Gaurish Dessai (Principal Enterprise Architect, SAP)

📅 Session 4: Enterprise Architects’ Guide on SAP BW Modernization

We then Discover SAP’s BW modernization assessment, which outlines how BW can integrate into the BDC ecosystem and support your digital transformation journey. You will get a complete understanding of how you can transform your SAP Business Warehouse landscape.

  • When: June 19, 2025, at 11 AM EST / 5 PM CET
  • Speakers:
    • Jason Luo (Principal Enterprise Architect, SAP)
    • Gaurish Dessai (Principal Enterprise Architect, SAP)

📅 Session 5: Planning Your Transition Paths to SAP Business Data Cloud

Transitioning to SAP Business Data Cloud is crucial for harmonizing cloud operations, enabling faster product innovation cycles, and improving resilience. By transitioning to SAP Business Data Cloud, organizations can leverage a unified data platform that supports impactful decision-making and drives innovation. This session will guide you through the strategic roadmap and practical steps to ensure a smooth and successful transition. You’ll also learn about a range of complimentary SAP assessments and services designed to support your journey and facilitate smooth collaboration as you move to the Business Data Cloud.

When: June 26th, 2025, at 11 AM EST / 5 PM CET

  • Speakers:
    • Naveen Dumpa (Enterprise Architect, SAP)
    • Jason Luo (Principal Enterprise Architect, SAP)

📅 Session 6: SAP Business Data Cloud and Databricks – Stronger Together

We are then joined by our partner’s Databricks to talk about the details of how SAP and Databricks partnership is a gamechanger for organizations who are looking to transform the data. At the core of this collaboration is SAP Databricks on Business Data Cloud, a first party offering that brings Databricks’ leading Data Science, Data Warehousing, and AI capabilities into the SAP ecosystem. With SAP data natively accessible in Databricks through Unity Catalog and enriched with business-ready metadata, this solution enables customers to seamlessly harness the power of their most valuable data—SAP—on the industry's best data platform, delivering both immediate insights and long-term transformation.

  • When: July 10, 2025, at 11 AM EST / 5 PM CET
  • Speakers:
    • Qi Su (Solution Architect, Databricks)
    • Michelle Wiktorsson (Manager, Field Engineering, Databricks)

📅 Session 7: SAP Business Data Cloud & Databricks in Action with Demos

See SAP Business Data Cloud and Databricks in action. Join us for the detailed demos of BDC Capabilities and Databricks to understand how this all comes alive together!

  • When : July 10th at 11 AM EST / 5 PM CET

Speakers:

  • Michelle Wiktorsson (Manager, Field Engineering, Databricks)
  • Gaurish Dessai (Principal Enterprise Architect, SAP)
  • Rithivika Acharya (Senior Product Specialist, SAP Business Data Cloud )

📅 Session 8: SAP BDC in Review – Insights, Learnings, and the Road Ahead

Join us for the final wrap-up session of the SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC), where we’ll bring together key insights from across all sessions—including the architectural foundations of BDC, SAP’s strategic partnership with Databricks, BW modernization strategies, live demos, and practical transition guidance. This interactive discussion will reflect on lessons learned, highlight real-world applications of BDC, and explore how organizations can become truly data-driven by leveraging a unified, governed, and AI-ready data platform.

  • When: July 24, 2025, at 11 AM EST / 5 PM CET
  • Speakers:
    • Paul Kurchina
    • Timo Elliott
    • Ralph Richter
    • Gaurish Dessai

Check out the registration page for the whole series using this link : https://go4.events.sap.com/shift-eaa-bdc-webinar/en_us/home.html

From building a data-driven organization to unleashing AI capabilities, modernizing SAP BW, partnering with Databricks, and planning your transition to SAP BDC — each session is packed with insights, demos, and practical advice. Whether you’re just starting or looking to accelerate your data transformation, this series is your ultimate guide to unlocking new possibilities with SAP.

Register now and get ready to transform your data future!! 

If there are any questions you want answered by the experts during our live sessions, please feel free to leave them in the comments below!!

Registration link for the whole series: https://go4.events.sap.com/shift-eaa-bdc-webinar/en_us/home.html

entry.title: The SAP Extended Warehouse Management FAQ Series: Migration to S/4HANA EWM on Private Cloud
entry.content:
What tools are available for S/4HANA EWM migration?
To migrate EWM master data and customizing, we provide 34 migration objects via the SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit. The SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit allows migration per warehouse number using pre-configured migration objects. Here is an overview of the available EWM migration objects via SAP Help Migration Objects for SAP S/4HANA.

The migration tool provides the options for direct data transfer, the usage of staging tables, as well as file-upload.

To migration from SAP EWM (e.g. 9.5) to EWM on SAP S/4HANA you can use the 29 migration object for direct data transfer. 

For migration from LE-WM to EWM on SAP S/4HANA you can use the 5 migration objects that enable migration via staging tables, availables in since SAP S/4HANA 2023, FPS1:

  • Warehouse Fixed Bin Assignment
  • Warehouse Stock
  • Warehouse Storage Bin
  • Warehouse Storage Bin Sorting

Both direct data transfer and staging table approaches enable the migration process with high amount of data, like warehouse stock, for optimized error handling throughout the migration process (upload, prepare, mapping, simulate, migrate).

Find further information via SAP Note 2976757 - SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit: Central Note for Transfer Data Directly from SAP System - SAP EWM to EWM based on SAP S/4HANA incl. Migration Guide.

The SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit is included in SAP S/4HANA licenses and shipment. Learn more about the SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit via SAP Blog Starter Blog for SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit or Part 1: Migrate your Data – Migration Cockpit (SAP S/4HANA 2020 and higher and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition), Migrate data using staging tables and methods for populating the staging tables with data.

I am a LE-WM customer, and we would like to know the possible migration path towards S/4, what options would we have?
There are three major aspects to consider, evaluating a transformation:

  • Target Application Architecture evaluation
  • Target Technical Architecture evaluation
  • Transformation Roadmap planning

Every aspect has its own depths to consider generally and specifics relevant to customer situations, e.g. business transformation, system landscape, roll-out strategies, etc. We would like to highlight in the following the most general and important considerations relevant to our customers planning their transformation from LE-WM solution.

With regards to Transformation Roadmap planning, the first step as from an LE-WM customer perspective, is to categorize your existing WM manage warehouses into different groups, e.g. by size, warehouse process complexity, level of automation, nature of the warehouse (e.g. manufacturing or parts/after-sales warehouse, distribution centers).Clustering can help you in structuring the discussion on rollout sequence, instance strategy, deployment strategy and assessing which target warehouse management solution is most suitable.

Consider SAP’s release strategy when choosing the target warehouse management solution. Please have a look at SAP Blog The SAP Extended Warehouse Management FAQ Series: Release Strategy for Warehouse Management highlighting the maintenance strategy per warehouse solution.

When it comes to migration of LE-WM contents, there are contents and guides available, depending on the target solution:

  • LE-WM to Stock Room Management
    • Perform an assessment on warehouse numbers via report STOCKROOM_COMPLIANCE_CHECK (see also SAP Note 2882809 - Scope Compliance Check for Stock Room Management) on Stock Room Management compliance (usage on functionality out-of-scope).
    • Review if result indicates necessity for local adjustments (e.g. example customizing or non-productive pilot processes) and decide which warehouse numbers can stay on Stock Room Management and which ones have to be transformed to EWM.
    • There is no technical migration needed to go from LE-WM to Stockroom Management.
  • LE-WM to EWM for SAP S/4HANA
    • Decide whether to transform to a decentral or embedded EWM on SAP S/4HANA.
    • EWM on SAP S/4HANA has different solution architecture requiring basic process customizing to be setup.
    • The following migration objects are available:
      • Storage types, storage sections, bin types, picking areas, doors, and staging areas (via tool Migrate Warehouse Layout Settings)
      • Storage bin data, including storage bin sorting for picking and crossline stock putaway (via SAP Migration Cockpit – File Upload)
      • Warehouse number and storage type-specific material master data, including fixed bin assignment, warehouse UoM and palletization data (via Implementation Tool for Warehouse Integration)
      • Stock data (via SAP Migration Cockpit – File Upload)
      • Physical Inventory completeness data (report Physical Inventory Completeness Migration - /SCWM/MIG_PI_COMPL)
      • Queues (via Mmigration tool for Cross-Process Settings - /SCWM/MIG_CROSS)
      • Movement types (via Mmigration tool for Cross-Process Settings - /SCWM/MIG_CROSS)
      • Stock removal strategies, putaway strategies, and replenishment strategies (via Mmigration tool for Cross-Process Settings - /SCWM/MIG_STRATEGIES)
    • Out-of-Scope: Transactional Data Migration, System Users/Authorization Roles and Profiles, Customizing, Customer-specific objects and enhancements.
    • Find further information on migration from LE-WM to EWM via SAP Note 3523591 - Migration from LE-WM to EWM on S/4HANA - Migration Guide.

An updated version of LE-WM to S/4HANA EWM migration guide is available via SAP Note 3523591

We are running EWM 9.x on business suite/On Premise, what I shall take into consideration in choosing the deployment option – embedded vs. decentral - for EWM on SAP S/4HANA?
There are various aspects to consider, choosing deployment strategy for warehouse management. The typical considerations our customers share with us are listed in the following:

  • Functional Coverage – Restrictions and Differences:
    • Close or loose integration required for
      • Transportation Management
      • Production Processes
      • Quality Management
      • Synchronous Goods Movement Postings (e.g. for Repetitive Manufacturing) required?
  • Operations and Maintenance:
    • Same upgrade and maintenance cycles possible for ERP and Logistics solutions?
    • Integration to multiple ERP-systems required.
    • Complexity and volume of warehouse operations combined with warehouse automation integration.
  • Project and Transformation – Risk and Costs:
    • Evaluation of hardware/subscription to cloud services costs for 1 –tier or 2-tier SAP system architecture.
    • Transformations risks considering changes from As-Is to Target Application Architecture – Training, Responsibilities, technology differences.
  • Project and Customer Specific Criteria:
    • Transformation motivation, e.g. system-consolidation, platform upgrade, back-to-standard, etc.
  • Preference of deployments depending on business requirements (e.g. production warehouse, distribution centers).

Feel free to have a read on the updated SAP Note 1606493 - SAP EWM Deployment Options Best Practices which talks about various consideration criteria when choosing the right S/4HANA EWM deployment options.

In short, there is no silver bullet which fits all, but rather must be analyzed by each individual case.

We are running EWM 9.x on business suite/On Premise, and we also have SAP ECC system(s), we want to incorporate the SAP S/4HANA project not only for EWM but also with ERP project, what shall we consider here?
We have learned from our customers that while planning the SAP S/4HANA EWM migration project, you might have to also adjust the project timeline with your SAP S/4HANA - ERP transformation and rollout.

The relevant transformation roadmap can be categorized into the following patterns:

  • ERP transformation first
  • EWM/logistic transformation first
  • Big bang

In practice, we have seen customers using either approach, and the decision criteria are typically resource commitment, project timeline and scope complexity/definition.

For example, if a customer chooses to have EWM transformation first and the project is running under tight schedule, we might want to keep the migration scope lean, so the project timeline is manageable from the start till go live. Then we are more independent for adapting and exploring new process capabilities in the new EWM on SAP S/4HANA system.

What if we also have some industrial add-ons on our SAP EWM 9.x?
S/4HANA EWM is restricted for all SAP industry solutions except for those features which are explicitly documented and supported by EWM. You may also want to go through the what’s new documentation to explore the details of supported scenario.

There are some known changes to SAP Industry Solutions (IS) features that where available with SAP ECC to SAP EWM integration and are transformed through the general change to integrate industry features natively to SAP S/4HANA:

  • Fashion Management: The EWM AddOn SAP EWM FOR FASHION 1.0 is not supported in EWM on SAP S/4HANA. Due to central changes in material/article master integration and delivery processing, used features have to be analyzed within the transformation project to understand which functionality has to be transformed to SAP Standard and which features have to be adapted via project solution.

For further information please read the release information notes of the relevant SAP S/4HANA release restrictions (e.g. EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2023, Private Cloud: SAP Note 3376453 - SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS00, FPS01, FPS02 and FPS03: Release information and restrictions for EWM in SAP S/4HANA, SAP Note3376455 - Release information and restrictions of Decentralized EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS00, FPS01, FPS02 and FPS03) on the restrictions.

With the upcoming EWM on SAP S/4HANA release we aim to introduce and enhance industry features e.g. for Automotive, Aerospace and Defence, Retail, Fashion Management and more to support the specific requirements of key industries.

We are already running a decentralized S/4HANA EWM on Private Cloud, and we would like to get support to migrate some warehouses to embedded S/4HANA EWM with ERP, what options would we have?
Currently the migration from decentral EWM on SAP S/4HANA to embedded EWM on SAP S/4HANA is not supported via the SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit (see also SAP Note 2976757 - SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit: Central Note for Transfer Data Directly from SAP System - SAP EWM to EWM based on SAPS/4HANA – attachment 2022_Migration_Guide_V17.pdf - Chapter 1.5 Scenarios not in Scope).

We are still running EWM 9.x on business suite/On Premise, considering the potential impact and timeline, can we do a lift and shift of the EWM to the S/4HANA EWM on Private Cloud?
A rule of thumb of a successful migration project is to always keep the scope and project timeline in control. A minimal requirement here would be that the corresponding custom codes and customer defined processes are validated, and if needed refactored, against the target release of S/4HANA EWM on PCE, as there have been some technical changes in the new S/4 systems. Please also plan sufficient resources and efforts into the test of end-to-end processes to ensure the business continuity after the migration.

We recommend our customers get familiar with all the new features and functionalities that are available in the SAP Roadmap Explorer and plan the process optimization accordingly into the future project timelines.

Customers can also contact your SAP Account team to get a closer look into available Premium Engagement service offerings as well as SAP consulting services on the EWM migration support.

In wrapping up our exploration of the topic, we would like to include the following SAP Blogs for further information around the topic of strategy, product versions and more:

  • Transformation to SAP S/4HANA for Warehouse and Tr... - SAP Community
  • Handling of open documents while migrating to SAP ... - SAP Community
  • SAP S/4HANA Private Edition EWM/TM Upgrade - SAP Community
  • Premium Hub CoE – DSC Knowledge Bits – New Migrati... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Calculation of the result field in Usage Decision
entry.content:
Hi,

Yes, you can change the result calculation method in SAP QM by configuring the summarization rule (e.g., average, min, max) in the inspection plan or master inspection characteristic. For advanced needs like weighted averages, implement custom logic using user exits (e.g., QEVA0001) or BAdIs. These allow full control over how sample results are evaluated before the Usage Decision.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Choosing the Right Analytical Tool: A Comprehensive Guide for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi @GiriRaaj,

Sure, although I have created lots of SAC dashboards that also included more detailed data (after drilling down). Also, we have the Data Analyzer in SAC which is created for ad-hoc deep-dives from aggregated results to the very details. 

But, this is all besides the point I'm making: Why offer multiple analytical solutions if your (embedded) SAC can also already feature all of this, and in case it really doesn't, why not extent the SAC functionality? With the current approach you are ending up with different tools, interfaces and a lot of overlap in functionality that requires matrices as detailed in this blog. Good luck explaining this to end-users.

Cheers,
Xavier

entry.title: Re: Hi, can we restrict Particular PO type creation, with...
entry.content:
Hi,

You can't restrict a specific PO type from being created without a PR using OMET alone—it applies system messages generally. To restrict by PO type, implement BAdI ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST with custom logic in PROCESS_HEADER.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Project Kernseife - improving the measurement of Clean Core in ABAP
entry.content:
Hi @robinthakral! 

The idea of the "noAPI" list is that SAP explicitly collects objects that must not be used in custom code.

On the example of RFC_READ_TABLE, there are several issues known to this function module: See Note 382318 as starting point. That's why I like it as example, because there is (e.g. for security reasons) clear recommendation already available on why not to use this approach, but to look for proper APIs.

Regarding the approach and meaning of the "noAPI", watch out the next weeks / months, as the whitepapers on clean core extensibility are currently reworked including these details.

entry.title: Re: SAP data migration
entry.content:
Hi Nehad,

If you are looking for the available objects for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, they are consolidated here https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/20607b6de19a4d34a41b0abec6186b01.html?version=2502.500 

You may filter for the migration approach "Staging table" or "Direct transfer" depending on your needs as some objects may be available for one approach while not for the other.

For assistance there is also sample data for many migration objects for "File / staging" approach. You may download the prefilled sample XMLs for the objects that are available from the following page https://help.sap.com/docs/DATAMIG_S4HANA/d5aa3caabab04bb68d9cc3352d003330/b8d3c4714d324d3182532a3f6ce63abb.html 

Kind regards,
Zsombor

entry.title: Re: Amount field length extension (AFLE) & Limitations in SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Hi Guidogioa!

The workarounds are the ones already provided by SAP that you have detailed above.

Unfortunately, we all believed that S4 would come with 23 digits in every CURR field and in every module, but it is not at all like that. You need to activate AFLE for the 23 digits and only works for FI postings... so useless in complex processes in big companies.

We have similar issues in Venezuela, Colombia, Lebanon, and the approach that is works better for us is by splitting POs or SO and/or invoices, specially if you are using additional local currencies like in USD or EUR. You could also combine all the other options too.

Hope this helps!

Good luck,

Mercedes

entry.title: Empowering Business Users: Unleashing the Power of Key User Extensibility in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
What Is Key User Extensibility?

It is the suite of browser‑based tools built into SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud that lets designated “key users” personalize and extend standard applications, data structures, and business rules—completely code‑free. All extensions are upgrade‑safe, maintainable, and governed by role‑based authorizations.

Simplifying the Business User Experience

UI Adaptation at Runtime

Key users enter Adaptation mode directly within the Fiori launchpad to hide, rename, reposition, or add fields and UI elements on standard apps—no ABAP or UI5 coding required. Once saved, changes persist through quarterly upgrades, keeping the interface aligned with user needs.

Custom Fields & Logic

The Custom Fields app allows users to introduce new fields (for example, adding “StockOwner” to the Warehouse Stock report), then publish these across UIs, reports, analytics, and APIs instantly. In parallel, the Custom Logic app (powered by BRFplus) lets users define simple rules—defaults, validations, derivations—without writing code.

Custom Business Objects

When unique data entities are needed—such as “CycleCountSchedule” for non‑standard count plans—key users define Custom Business Objects. The tool automatically generates database tables, OData services, Fiori list/object pages, and API endpoints, making custom masters behave like standard ones.

In‑App Business Rules Framework

For conditional logic or simple automations (e.g., “If |PostedQty – PickedQty| > 5, set TransferOrderStatus to ‘Review’”), the in‑app BRFplus interface enables rule creation, testing, and assignment within the Custom Logic app—keeping business logic close to the process.

Use Cases (Warehouse‑Focused Scenarios)

I am providing below warehouse specific use cases but similar use cases can be extended to other LoBs as well.

  1. Temperature‑Sensitive Handling Units
    A cold‑chain operator adds a “Temperature Range” field to the Handling Unit context, making it visible in packing UIs and IoT integrations for real‑time monitoring.
  2. Tailored Warehouse Monitor Views
    The control‑tower team hides unnecessary columns and reorders critical fields (SKU, Available Qty, Storage Type, Quality Status) via UI Adaptation, then saves a “Quality Check” variant—streamlining exception handling.
  3. Custom Cycle Count Schedule Object
    Inventory leads create a CBO named “CycleCountSchedule,” automatically generating a Fiori app and APIs that mobile workers use to pull active count plans and report results via handheld scanners.
  4. Auto‑Block Transfers on Quantity Mismatch
    A rule in BRFplus blocks any transfer order when the posted vs. picked quantity delta exceeds five units, flagging it for review and preventing inventory inconsistencies.
  5. Enhanced Warehouse‑Stock Report
    The team adds “StockOwner” via Custom Fields and includes it in the embedded analytics query—enabling stock‑by‑owner analysis (in‑house vs. 3PL) directly in Fiori dashboards.

Governance & Security

All extensibility tools are controlled by role‑based authorizations, ensuring only users assigned the Key User Extensibility role can access Adapt UI, Custom Fields, Custom Business Objects, or Custom Logic apps—maintaining strict change management and audit trails.

Upgrade Safety & Integration

SAP’s cloud‑native extensibility framework preserves all in‑app modifications across quarterly releases, eliminating upgrade rework. Standard SAP code remains untouched through a clear separation of core and extensions via published APIs and extension points.

Business Impact

  • Faster Time‑to‑Market: Key users implement changes in days, not weeks.
  • Reduced IT Backlog: Frees developers for strategic initiatives.
  • Higher User Adoption & Satisfaction: Personalized UIs and processes.
  • Continuous Innovation: Rapid experimentation without technical debt.

Who Benefits?

  • Business Users craving tailored experiences
  • Consultants empowering clients for self‑sufficiency
  • Decision‑Makers driving agility and cost efficiency

Next Steps

  1. Open the Custom Fields app to add a sample field.
  2. Launch Adapt UI in your Fiori Launchpad and save a variant.
  3. Explore Custom Business Objects in the Extensibility Library.

SAP Help Portal Links

  • Adapt UI at Runtime: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/4fc8d03390c342da8a60f8ee387bca1a/5c424437bf794f809087fdce391149f2.html
  • Custom Fields & Logic: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/57909455bf7c4fdd8bcf48d76c1eae33.html
  • Creating Custom Fields: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/3a713139e548434e8a50800e7a7c8172.html
  • Custom Business Objects: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/b45696ca0d9143cba040797e9c71aa44.html
  • Create Custom Business Object (Extensibility Explorer): https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_EXTENSIBILITY_EXPLORER/b0e8d558ba2f47f5b02a3fc0ac9edc34/6bd534db33694eba9ddde788ef717dfd.html
  • Business Rule Framework plus: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/8b78ae42ec1c4b75937806fcd4fd7d1c/9a6b67ce7c26446483af079719edf679.html
  • Enabling UI Adaptation at Runtime (Admin): https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_BW4HANA/a7b390faab1140c087b8926571e942b7/402b392585834dd38dd381d8f04b2616.html
  • Adapting the UI of an SAP Fiori App at Runtime: https://help.sap.com/docs/UI_ADD-ON_FOR_SAP_NETWEAVER_20/17ae0e97e0fc424a9c368f350c0ba6bd/5c424437bf794f809087fdce391149f2.html
  • Custom Logic App: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/3aa57e5b9a9f46218ef8cc38d703c8f4.html

entry.title: Nominal -/Original Value in Credit-Risk Based Impairment
entry.content:
Dear All, 

when executing the run for "Post Credit-Risk Based Impairment" the result list shows for e.g. as nominal -/original value 541,90€

However the line item report and hence the posting document shows a different value.

When trying to reconcile the difference between amounts shown in the result list vs. the posting document like net amount, discounts and so on there is no way to explain the amount shown as Nominal -/Original Value in Credit-Risk Based Impairment. The differene only occurs with accounts subject to VAT taxation, nevertheless it is not possible to match the amounts given in the result list

Does anyone know how the system is determining the amount?

entry.title: Re: Choosing the Right Analytical Tool: A Comprehensive Guide for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi Xavier /@xjhacking 

When we embedded SAC back in 2019 into S/4HANA Cloud the intend was not to only focus on SAC Stories. Each 'analytical tool' has its benefits depending on the use case and eSAC Stories (so far) would not be able to replace them. Some of the above listed is historically grown and heavily used by our customers.

But yes, you are right telling us your concerns. Still, looking at the new innovations with MDA or Review Booklet, we achieve improved consistency across our analytic capabilities by reusing the same controls and access libraries in Fiori/UI5 as we use with SAC.

entry.title: Re: Automatically Generate Packaging Items in EWM
entry.content:
Thanks for the blog entry. 
What about a scenario where you need to store the packaging materials in the rack and then do a replenishment to the GM bin?

 

e.g. 3 pallets of packaging material car6 with 270pc p.pal. arrive in the warehouse and have to be stored in the rack until needed. one day, the GM bin runs low and needs REPL, can packaging materials be stored in rack and then REPL (automatically or manually) to the GM bin so that they can be used and postet GI?

At the moment the solution would be to create a dummy material for each packaging material so that this can be stored and REPL and then scrapped while the whole stock of the original packaging material is on the GM bin, but that seems unnessecarily complicated and prone to errors with stock keeping.

entry.title: BAPI_COSTCENTER_GETLIST1 how parameters *FROM and *TO work?
entry.content:
The function module BAPI_COSTCENTER_GETLIST1 has several parameters doubled, one with suffix FROM, one with suffix TO, e.g. COSTCENTER_FROM and COSTCENTER_TO. I understand that they are intervals but isn't it possible to select all values easily or one exact value or all values starting with a constant literal?

The doubled parameters suffixed FROM and TO (instead of * below) are:

  • COSTCENTER_*
  • COMPANYCODE_*
  • PERSON_IN_CHARGE_*
  • DATE_*
  • BUSINESS_AREA_*
  • PERSON_IN_CHARGE_USER_*

Thank you.

Sandra

entry.title: Re: BAPI_COSTCENTER_GETLIST1 how parameters *FROM and *TO...
entry.content:
All the parameters except DATE_* work the same:

FROMTO# of lines in the range (zero =select all values)SIGNOPTIONLOWHIGH
EmptyEmptyZeroIN/AN/AN/A
Not emptyNot emptyOneIBTFROMTO
Contains *EmptyOneICPFROM 
Not empty and not contains *EmptyOneIEQFROM 
EmptyNot emptyOneILETO 

The logic for all 5 parameters can be found in the subroutine BAPI_FILL_SELECTION_TABLE of the function group KMA1, and the logic for initializing the range can be found in the subroutine BCF_APPEND_COSEL_LINE.

Concerning the two parameters DATE_FROM and DATE_TO, they work as an interval except if they are left empty, it's the interval from 19000101 to 99991231.

entry.title: Check Horizon Zero Confirmation and BPS scheduling with Working Times getting unconfirmed quantities
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am currently facing an issue with sales orders not getting confirmed when Scope of aATP is defined with Check Horizon - Zero Confirmation (see below) and BPS is active for scheduling with Working Times assigned to specific activities (Load).

Although Scheduling is working as expected and considering the activities that have been defined and attributes durations and working times when maintained, I am facing an issue with Sales Orders quantities not getting confirmed.

1. BPS is active and activity Load has been set up with durations and working time

2. Activity Pick maintained only with durations

3. There is plenty of stock available for this material

4. Check horizon is 14 days (end lead time: 02.06.2025). So anything before that date should be confirmed with current stocks. However, see below for the ATP screen (no quantity confirmed)

This seems to be somehow affected by the Workime Time assigned to activity load for the characteristic combination assigned. 

The working time was defined with shifts on Mondays,  Tuesdays and Wednesdays of 1 minute, meaning in this case that GI can only happen at 18h00 on these 3 days.

From a Scheduling perspective, everything works perfectly fine (as you can see above). However, Availability Check is not confirming quantities with the Zero Confirmation Scope Horizon setup as it seems to get in conflict with the working times assigned in scheduling for later activities. However, this should not be relevant as Material Availability Date is always the date taken into account to determine availability.

Any idea or can someone please provide some inputs? 

Thanks a lot!!!

entry.title: Re: invalid path for report file
entry.content:
Hi,

when this happens next time please see if SAP Note 3151730 relates to your case and you have 74 files in temp folder.

3151730 - Invalid Report File Path with Crystal Reports
Component: SBO-REP-CR (SAP Business One > Reporting > SAP Crystal Reports for SAP Business One), Version: 1, Released On: 29.04.2022
SAP Business One Notes
Symptom
Invalid Report File Path error appears when you preview or print a certain amount of Crystal reports from SAP Business One.
Reproducing the issue
Prerequisites:
You are using SAP Business One 10.0 FP2111 and are printing documents with Crystal Reports.
Scenario:
1.
Go to Sales A/R → Document Printing.
2.
Choose any document type and choose OK. Make sure you have more than 74 records in the Document Printing list.
3.
Choose at least 75 documents and choose Print.
Expected behavior:
All documents are printed successfully.
Actual behavior:
74 documents are printed successfully and the remaining documents are not executed successfully with error returned: Invalid Report File Path

 

BR,

Matija

entry.title: Re: Quering document lines in user define values
entry.content:
Hi,

try using this FMS in an nvarchar field and see if it populates correctly. If yes, that means your result is coming back in a different type than expected, so most likely you need to cast it as integer or use substitute to change (usually its problems with comma(,) and dot(.) being misinterpreted).

BR,

Matija

entry.title: What's New in SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition 2505?
entry.content:
SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition is an SaaS solution on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) that is used to govern core attributes of business partner data (more domains planned). Companies can use it to kickstart a master data management initiative at low entry barriers. For details, see also the SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition overview and deep-dive presentations.

What's New?

Continuing the blog series about innovations in SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition, I'd like to provide you with details on the latest innovations with SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition 2505. New features come in the areas of data quality management and SOAP replication of business partner.​

Data Quality Management

New features in the Validation Rules for Business Partners app​ include:

  • Delete the validation rules' dependencies to other objects (for example, simulations, dimensions, or data quality evaluation runs) when deleting rules​
  • Disable several validation rules at once​
  • Filter for enabled usages, disabled usages and usages in general​

SOAP Replication

The enhanced SOAP replication of business partners includes:

  • ​Replication of business partners with their relationships​
  • Selective replication of specific business partner roles​
  • Automatic retriggering of business partner relationship messages

Workflow Configuration

Note: As of release 2505, you must configure a mail destination to send notifications. To do this, create a mail destination in your subaccount, as described in Configure Workflow Capability Mail Destination.

For a complete overview of all updates, see the latest What's New Viewer - SAP Master Data Governance, Cloud Edition.

To stay on track, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance, read the MDG-related blogs, and join the  Q&A forum.

 

 

Best,

Markus

​
